'''Lee Kershaw''' (born 2 May 1999) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Huddersfield Giants]], on a one-week loan from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and at [[Castleford Tigers]] in the Super League.

==Background==
Kershaw was born in [[Bradford, West Yorkshire]], [[England]].

He played [[rugby union]] as a junior for Bingley and West Yorkshire, before moving into playing [[rugby league|league]] for the Eastmoor Dragons and Keighley Albion.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://grmsport.com/blogfeed/2020/6/30/lee-kershaw-grassroots-to-greatness |title=LEE KERSHAW - GRASSROOTS TO GREATNESS? |website=GRM Sport |date=30 June 2020 |url-status=usurped |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111192223/https://grmsport.com/blogfeed/2020/6/30/lee-kershaw-grassroots-to-greatness |archive-date=11 January 2024}}</ref>

==Career==
===Wakefield Trinity===
In 2019, Kershaw made his [[Super League]] début for [[Wakefield Trinity|Trinity]] against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921737|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 26-24 Leeds Rhinos|publisher=BBC}}</ref>

Kershaw played 17 matches for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
In early February, it was reported that Kershaw was training with [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] however he was not part of their squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/london-broncos-lee-kershaw-wakefield-31880592/|title=Ex-Wakefield Trinity star Lee Kershaw on life as a free agent and London Broncos battle|website=www.mirror.co.uk}}</ref>
On 9 February 2024, he completed his move to the newly-promoted [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=9 Feb 2024|title=Ex-Wakefield Trinity winger Lee Kershaw signs permanent Super League deal with newly-promoted London Broncos|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-wakefield-trinity-winger-lee-kershaw-signs-permanent-super-league-deal-with-newly-promoted-london-broncos/}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2024 Super League season, Kershaw made his club debut for London in their 40-4 loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304742|title=Super League: St Helens 40-4 London Broncos - Saints thrash promoted side|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 4, Kershaw scored two tries in London's 60-22 loss against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68501497|title=Super League: London Broncos 22-60 Wigan Warriors - reigning champions go top again|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
In November 2024, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] confirmed that Kershaw would remain in Super League for 2025 after signing with the East Yorkshire club.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=1 November 2024|access-date=3 November 2024|title=Hull KR sign Broncos winger Kershaw|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/c7748ezxymko}}</ref>

==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ====
On 19 February 2025, it was announced that Kershaw would join [[Castleford Tigers]] on an initial two-week loan.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=19 February 2025 |title=Hull KR winger makes Super League switch following off-season move |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-winger-makes-super-league-switch-following-off-season-move |access-date=19 February 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> After making two appearances, he returned to Hull KR following a spate of injuries.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Craven |first=David |date=6 March 2025 |title=Super League coach left fed-up with two week loan deals |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/danny-mcguire-castleford-tigers-loan-1013520 |access-date=18 May 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref>

In April, he was resigned by Castleford for a second two-week loan spell, with [[Innes Senior]] suspended for their upcoming fixture against [[Leigh Leopards]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=7 Apr 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Castleford Tigers re-sign Super League winger ahead of Leigh Leopards clash|url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-re-sign-super-league-winger-ahead-of-leigh-leopards-clash/}}</ref> After his third Tigers appearance, head coach [[Danny McGuire]] admitted it was unlikely Kershaw would remain on a longer deal as Castleford's contracted wingers returning from injury would be preferred.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=11 April 2025 |title=Castleford coach outlines Lee Kershaw transfer stance as fresh injury revealed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-coach-outlines-lee-kershaw-transfer-stance-as-fresh-injury-revealed |access-date=18 May 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

====Huddersfield Giants (loan)====
On 11 May 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] on one-week loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 May 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Huddersfield Giants land Hull KR winger in immediate boost|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-land-hull-kr-winger-in-immediate-boost}}</ref>
